channel.title: go on David's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in go on David's Blog

item.title: Caching in Go with Redis - Reading Notes
item.description: Source: Go-Redis Caching: Strategies, Best Practices & Common Pitfalls 1. What is Caching? At its core, caching is the practice of storing a copy of data temporarily in a location for faster access upon subsequent requests. Imagine reading a thick encyclopedia. Instead of going through the entire book every time you need to reference a specific topic, you might keep a note or bookmark on pages of interest. Caching in the world of web applications follows a similar principle.
